title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC made its NWSL debut at Gillette Stadium, playing against Gotham FC before over 30,000 fans.
Gotham FC defeated Legacy FC 1-0, with the match featuring an NWSL record-tying eight yellow cards.
Legacy FC head coach Filipa Patão emphasized her team's aggressive play, while Gotham's coach praised their calm under pressure.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off their St. Patrick's Day shows with a lively performance at MGM Music Hall at Fenway.
The band addressed political topics, dedicating songs to immigrants and criticizing ICE during their March 13 concert.
Dropkick Murphys played a mix of old and new songs, including 'Barroom Hero' and 'Citizen I.C.E.', with special guests.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett aims to support Joslin Diabetes Center, which helped him manage his Type 1 diabetes diagnosis at age 11.
His participation highlights a personal journey, overcoming early fears about playing sports and living with diabetes.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter receives her fifth Oscar nomination for 'Sinners', becoming the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
Carter made history as the first Black person to win the Oscar for Best Costume Design in 2019.
'Sinners' received 16 nominations; Carter sourced costumes from Massachusetts and used local manufacturers.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools may face over 200 layoffs if a proposed property-tax override isn't approved by voters.
The school district needs $18.5 million over three years to avoid cuts to staffing and programs.
The override vote will take place during Brookline's annual town election on May 5.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops to reduce puppy mill sales.
The legislation proposes partnerships between pet stores and animal shelters to facilitate adoptions instead of sales.
The PETS Act seeks to address housing and insurance barriers, promoting accessibility to pet ownership in Massachusetts.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts faces intensified drought despite significant February snowfall and ongoing snow melts.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts declared Level 3 Critical Drought; Connecticut River Valley at Level 2 Significant Drought.
Officials recommend water conservation measures, highlighting leakage checks, efficient appliances, and mindful water usage.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay explores why some women are obscuring their boyfriends on Instagram, focusing on new relationship dynamics.
The essay, which went viral, sparked discussions about modern relationships and challenges to traditional models of heterosexuality.
Joseph's insights suggest women might prioritize independence over coupling with men, challenging societal expectations in heterosexual partnerships.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp offers children with serious medical conditions a safe place to experience fun and camaraderie.
O'Neill's motivation is personal, as his fiancée's sister, a camp alum, is now a counselor in remission.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles will not trade wide receiver A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots at this time.
Trade discussions for A.J. Brown could resume closer to June 1, when salary cap relief could benefit the Eagles.
The Patriots reportedly offered a first- and third-round draft pick for A.J. Brown, which the Eagles declined.
